articles.title: Top-5 valued firms add Rs 1.54 lakh crore in market cap; TCS emerges biggest gainer
Gold, silver under pressure this week as US-Iran conflict, oil rally cloud outlook
Promised 200 Riyals a month, made to work 12–15 hours a day, no pay for four months: Hyderabad woman's Oman ordeal
articles.summary: Unable to endure the alleged abuse, Shabnam Begum reportedly escaped from her employer.

articles.title: Meet Amitabh Bachchan's former CA, who built a ₹28,000 crore pharma giant
articles.summary: Godha began his professional practice in 1971. Within a few years, he was handling the personal finances, taxation, and investments of Bollywood superstar Amitabh Bachchan and his family.

articles.title: Husband at 19, became a father at 21, and still scored 605 marks: The story of a Gorakhpur student's four-year journey
articles.summary: By day he attended coaching and taught tuition classes; by night he studied in a library until 11 pm.

articles.title: 'I left my ₹25,000 Gurgaon job': Auto driver says he now earns ₹45,000 a month and is using it to educate his three children
articles.summary: The driver said he earns ₹1,500–2,000 a day working eight to nine hours and uses the money to fund the education of his three children in the village.

articles.title: Viral wallet hack decoded: Can aluminum foil help block wireless card theft? Here's the fact
articles.summary: The unusual hack has sparked curiosity — and skepticism — about whether a thin sheet of kitchen foil can really protect against cyber-enabled pickpocketing.

articles.title: India-UK ties to remain strong under new PM Andy Burnham, say strategic experts
Pakistan trade crashes. Afghanistan pitches deeper economic ties with India
articles.summary: India-Afghanistan bilateral trade stood at $907.85 million in 2025-26 and has broadly remained around the $1 billion mark over the past five years

articles.title: 'Happy now, children?': Kerala declares school holiday for FIFA World Cup final, students get Monday off
articles.summary: Football has dominated conversations in Kerala throughout the tournament, with several ministers openly backing their favourite teams.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Azim Premji: ‘People have to take control of their own lives...’
articles.summary: Often referred to as the "Czar of the Indian IT Industry," he is best known for transforming Wipro Limited from a small family-run cooking oil business into a multi-billion-dollar global software and outsourcing giant.

articles.title: Mahadev app case: EBIX Group Chairman Vikas Garg given 10-days custody; how he used betting proceeds to buy foreign firms
articles.summary: The arrest follows a series of FIRs registered by police in Chhattisgarh, Andhra Pradesh, West Bengal, and other states against operators, promoters, and associates of these illegal betting platforms.

articles.title: Making waves underground: India's first undersea rail tunnel takes shape
articles.summary: India's first undersea rail tunnel excavation has commenced for the bullet train project. A giant Tunnel Boring Machine began its journey beneath Thane Creek on Saturday. This marks the start of excavation on the seven-kilometer undersea stretch.

articles.title: IRDAI launches comic book series to simplify life insurance for consumers
articles.summary: IRDAI chairman Ajay Seth unveiled a new comic book series to improve life insurance literacy. This initiative aims to simplify complex insurance concepts for the general public. The stories feature a young advisor explaining financial decisions and their long-term impact.

articles.title: Fans 1, Productivity 0: How FIFA World Cup 2026 could trigger $17bn loss worldwide
'Never a good time to call it a day…': Kotak Bank’s MD & CEO Ashok Vaswani on his decision to leave
articles.summary: Vaswani noted how the bank had done a lot of “heavy lifting” addressing the various challenges and was now ready to step up the accelerator, and therefore perhaps not a bad time for him to walk away.

articles.title: India's power sector set for strong FY27 growth on rising demand, capacity additions: Report
articles.summary: India's power sector anticipates sustained growth through fiscal year twenty twenty-seven. Surging electricity demand and record peak consumption fuel this positive outlook. Continued capacity additions, especially renewables, will strengthen utility performance.

articles.title: India's top private banks defy West Asia shadow with strong Q1 credit growth. Here's why
articles.summary: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank and Kotak Mahindra Bank reported double digit growth in advances in the April-June quarter. Executives pointed that impact from the West Asia conflict had been limited so far, but portfolios were being monitored continuously.

articles.title: India strengthens DTAA with Sri Lanka to plug anti avoidance loopholes
articles.summary: Introduces concept of Principal Purpose Test, treaty not to be used to grant benefit for double non taxation and treaty shopping

articles.title: Punjab National Bank Q1FY27 net profit up 213% to Rs 5253 crore
articles.summary: Punjab National Bank reported a substantial net profit increase for the quarter ending June 30, 2026. The bank's standalone net profit rose over 200 percent year-on-year. This growth was primarily driven by a sharp decline in tax outgo.

articles.title: ITR filing: Sold shares, property or crypto this year? Here's what you must know before filing your income tax return
articles.summary: With the Income tax department increasingly using technology, data analytics, AIS, broker reports, mutual fund disclosures and overseas information exchange, filing an accurate ITR now requires not only correct tax computation but also complete and consistent reporting.

articles.title: IDBI Bank Q1FY27 net profit rises 3.3% to Rs 2007 crore
articles.summary: IDBI Bank reported a 3.3% rise in standalone net profit for June 2026 quarter. Lower provisions supported earnings, offsetting a weaker operating profit. Net interest income saw a slight decline while non-interest income dropped significantly.

articles.title: Pakistan to revise fuel prices daily; current account slips into $139 million FY26 deficit
articles.summary: Pakistan will now revise fuel prices daily due to volatile global oil markets. This change aims to increase transparency in fuel pricing mechanisms for consumers. The nation also reported a current account deficit after experiencing a surplus last year.

articles.title: Nobody wants to wait on hold anymore. But can AI replace customer care?
Planning a mountain workation? This Blinkit delivery in Manali reveals one catch you can't ignore
articles.summary: A video by content creator Akhil Dhruv showed just how far quick-commerce has travelled, and what it still can't do.

articles.title: Why Gujarat beat Maharashtra, Tamil Nadu to become India’s No.1 investment state
articles.summary: Gujarat topped NITI Aayog’s first Investment Friendliness Index 2026 with a score of 56.6, beating Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu thanks to its strong infrastructure, faster business approvals, lower power costs and high exports.

articles.title: CBDT grants retrospective income tax exemption to BBNDA under Section 10(46)
articles.summary: The CBDT has granted the Baddi Barotiwala Nalagarh Development Authority (BBNDA) a retrospective income tax exemption under Section 10(46) of the Income-tax Act, 1961.

articles.title: New money? India's currency might soon get a plastic makeover
articles.summary: India's Reserve Bank is exploring polymer banknotes for enhanced durability. Manufacturers are invited to bid for specialized plastic substrate materials. Polymer notes offer greater resistance to moisture and dirt than paper.

articles.title: 'Not just planted a new tree in space, but...': PM Modi to Skyroot Aerospace founders after Vikram-1's successful launch
articles.summary: Vikram-1's maiden test flight Mission Aagaman lifted off from the First Launch Pad at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre in Andhra Pradesh's Sriharikota on Saturday.

articles.title: LNG traffic through the Strait of Hormuz falls sharply: S&P Global Energy
articles.summary: Laden LNG transits fall sharply after an attack on a QatarEnergy vessel while regional loadings remain resilient, but exports face renewed shipping constraints.

articles.title: Is India’s first hydrogen train cheaper than diesel or more expensive? Cost of running 1 km revealed
articles.summary: Building a hydrogen train costs around Rs 40-80 crore, making it nearly 30% more expensive than a conventional diesel train. The bigger savings come after it starts running.

articles.title: LPG, CNG, PNG rates today (July 18): Check latest prices in Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata and other cities
articles.summary: Another respite for the consumer is that the government has removed all restrictions on the supply of non-domestic packed LPG cylinders and restored supplies to the levels prevailing before the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: The future isn't more ACs? China is building comfort into its infrastructure instead of fighting heat
articles.summary: China is tackling intensifying heat by replacing individual air conditioners with district cooling networks tied to smart energy management—so whole neighborhoods share efficient, low-carbon cooling that eases grid strain and cuts emissions.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel prices today, July 18: Check latest prices in Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata and other cities
articles.summary: The petrol and diesel rates have remained stable since May 25, when state-owned fuel retailers last hiked petrol prices by ₹2.61 per litre and diesel by ₹2.71 per litre.

articles.title: 'Every business' dharma is profit': Vivek Oberoi explains the mindset behind 300% return
articles.summary: What does it take for a Bollywood star to quietly build a business portfolio that has delivered returns of up to 300%? For Vivek Oberoi, the answer lies in an early lesson in money, a sharp eye for opportunity, and a belief that profit and purpose can go hand in hand.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Lakshmi Mittal: ‘We are beginning to see the benefits...’
articles.summary: Lakshmi Mittal is an Indian billionaire industrialist widely known as the "King of Steel." He is the executive chairman of ArcelorMittal, the world's largest steel and mining company by output.

articles.title: Textile sector to sew loose ends as FTAs kick in
articles.summary: New free trade agreements offer Indian textile exporters significant tariff advantages. The UK deal eliminates up to twelve percent tariffs, boosting competitiveness against rivals. Companies anticipate increased orders and potential export growth over the next few years.

articles.title: NITI index: Gujarat, Maharashtra lead in wooing investors
Sensex soars 965 pts as Q1 results lift mood on Dalaal-Street
Pin code wars: Quick commerce cos add 900 dark stores in 3 months
'Front-running patterns seen in PMS by Sebi's AI tools'
articles.summary: Sebi is extensively deploying artificial intelligence tools for market surveillance. These advanced capabilities are generating actionable alerts for regulators. Patterns from these alerts suggest front-running is occurring within the PMS industry.

articles.title: RBI may look through price worries, high credit growth
articles.summary: RBI governor Sanjay Malhotra indicated no immediate policy reaction to current inflation. He described price pressures as contained, attributing them to supply-side factors. Growth remains resilient, supported by domestic demand and investment.

articles.title: Smartphone sales decline 10% in Q1, biggest in 3 years
articles.summary: Chinese brands feel the heat as below 15,000 segment plunges 45% in a year
